new-south-wales-jo-haylen-summer-hill.summary: As NSW Minister for Transport (2023 until Feb 2025), Haylen oversaw the opening of the Sydney Metro City & Southwest (M1) line on 19 August 2024, describing it as 'truly a historic moment' and saying 'What an incredible day to experience the first passengers travelling on the new M1 extension deep below the city and under the harbour.' She resigned the portfolio in Feb 2025.

sources.title: Sydney Metro: City celebrates 'historic' moment as multibillion dollar driverless line opens

new-south-wales-john-graham-statewide.summary: Graham became NSW Minister for Transport in February 2025 (after Jo Haylen's resignation), taking full carriage of the portfolio in March 2025. As minister he is responsible for the Sydney Metro program and has fronted budget estimates on Metro project delivery and delays, including the Metro Southwest (Bankstown line) conversion and Western Sydney Airport metro.

sources.title: John Graham (Australian politician)

new-south-wales-natalie-ward-statewide.summary: As NSW Shadow Minister for Transport (Liberal), Ward has repeatedly attacked the Minns Labor government over Sydney Metro delays. On 3 November 2025 she said of the Metro: 'Labor fought against it, and now they are failing to deliver it... Sydney deserves better than this,' and she has cast delays to the Western Sydney Airport metro, Metro West, Parramatta light rail and the City Metro extension as 'Labor's transport legacy'.

sources.title: All aboard the NSW Labor delay express

queensland-bart-mellish-aspley.summary: As Queensland's Labor Transport and Main Roads Minister until the Oct 2024 election (now Shadow Minister for Transport and Main Roads), Mellish defended Cross River Rail, telling Estimates in 2024 that 'the majority of construction on Cross River Rail is expected to be complete within 2025 and first passenger services are expected to commence in 2026' — a timeline the incoming Crisafulli LNP government later disputed as unachievable.

sources.title: Labor secures $17B for Cross River Rail project (Mickelberg statement citing Mellish's Estimates evidence)
sources.publisher: Mirage News (reproducing Qld Government media statement)

queensland-brent-mickelberg-buderim.summary: As Queensland's LNP Minister for Transport and Main Roads (from the Oct 2024 Crisafulli government), Mickelberg has attacked the former Labor government's delivery of Cross River Rail, alleging the project's cost has blown out to more than $17bn (later cited as up to ~$19bn) versus the original $5.4bn budget and would not be complete until 2029, and later announcing revised performance-based contractual arrangements to finish it.

sources.title: Labor secured more than $17 billion for Cross River Rail (Mickelberg media statement)
sources.publisher: Mirage News (reproducing Qld Government media statement)

south-australia-tom-koutsantonis-west-to.summary: As SA Minister for Infrastructure and Transport (2022 until Sept 2025), Koutsantonis was the political lead on the ~10.5km River Torrens to Darlington (T2D) tunnels project completing the North-South Corridor. In 2024 he committed to awarding the T2D Alliance contract by Q3 2024 with delivery 'on budget'; he has also acknowledged the project's original design 'had to be thoroughly reviewed and fixed' as an example of prior poor infrastructure planning.

sources.title: Creating South Australia's transport future, together

tasmania-michael-ferguson-bass.summary: As Tasmania's Minister for Infrastructure and Transport (2019 until August 2024, when he relinquished the portfolio), Ferguson was the political lead on the New Bridgewater Bridge, Tasmania's largest-ever transport infrastructure project. He called it his 'passion project' and said he 'oversaw construction and delivery' of the bridge, which opened to traffic in May 2025.

sources.title: Infrastructure Achievements
Michael Ferguson Resigns As Infrastructure Minister

victoria-brad-battin-berwick.summary: As Victorian Liberal Opposition Leader (from Dec 2024), Battin is an opponent of the Suburban Rail Loop. In January 2025 he announced he would halt further development of the SRL if he becomes Premier at the 2026 state election, and on 24 March 2025 the Liberal/National Coalition under his leadership publicly called on Premier Allan to immediately suspend works on the project before more taxpayer money is spent.

sources.title: Suburban Rail Loop

victoria-jacinta-allan-bendigo-east.summary: As Premier and long-time Minister for Transport Infrastructure and the Suburban Rail Loop (SRL), Allan is the leading champion of Victoria's Suburban Rail Loop. She has overseen the ~$34.5bn SRL East since its inception, signed the first tunnelling contracts, and continued to defend and fund the project (advancing tax measures and funding in December 2025), framing it as congestion-cutting rail linking Melbourne's middle-suburban universities and delivering tens of thousands of homes.

sources.title: Suburban Rail Loop
Works To Kick Off On The Suburban Rail Loop

victoria-jess-wilson-kew.summary: As Victorian Liberal Opposition Leader (from November 2025, deposing Battin), Wilson made opposition to the Suburban Rail Loop her first major policy position, reaffirming the party's opposition and committing to pause/halt all development of the SRL, including current construction, if she becomes Premier at the 2026 election.

sources.title: Suburban Rail Loop

western-australia-rita-saffioti-west-swa.summary: As WA Deputy Premier, Treasurer and Minister for Transport, Saffioti is the political owner and champion of METRONET, WA's largest-ever public transport investment (~72km of new rail and 23 new stations). She has repeatedly promoted its delivery, e.g. on a Sept 2024 milestone calling it 'testament to the enormous amount of planning and preparation that has gone into delivering this project', and on 14 Nov 2025 announcing the final Midland Station as 'the final piece of the METRONET puzzle'.

sources.title: Major milestone reached in METRONET for WA
sources.publisher: Prime Minister of Australia (joint media release)

sources.title: Completion date announced for final METRONET rail project

victoria-2026-steve-martin-benambra.summary: Says "I will fight for better and safer roads" for the Benambra region.

sources.title: Libs choose Steve Martin for Benambra

victoria-2026-owen-cosgriff-bendigo-east.summary: Says inadequate public transport is a policy failure, noting only 5% of Bendigo CBD trips use public transport at peak times, and argues Bendigo's compact size means most residents should be serviced by existing train lines or frequent buses.

sources.title: Owen Cosgriff — Victorian Socialists candidate for Bendigo East

victoria-2026-nalinda-amarasiri-gunaward.summary: Pledges to fight for 'better road infrastructure in our community' in the Berwick area.

sources.title: Nalinda Amarasiri Gunawardana — Victorian Socialists candidate for Berwick

victoria-2026-jessica-smith-brighton.summary: Advocates 'better-connected and higher-frequency public transport' so it is accessible to all.

sources.title: Jessica Smith — Victorian Socialists candidate for Brighton

victoria-2026-adam-pulford-brunswick.summary: Nominated more frequent trains on the Upfield line and traffic congestion on Sydney Road among the local issues of his Brunswick campaign.

sources.title: Greens opt for Pulford to contest state election

victoria-2026-dimitrios-tafidis-bulleen.summary: States that "The Bulleen District lacks crucial public transport and free community spaces".

sources.title: Dimitrios Tafidis - Victorian Socialists candidate for Bulleen

victoria-2026-annalise-mercer-cranbourne.summary: Lists public transport among her stated priorities, and says "people are feeling the pressures of rapid growth that has not been matched with the infrastructure and services we need".

sources.title: Annalise Mercer - Greens candidate for Cranbourne

victoria-2026-richard-farr-croydon.summary: States his priorities include "improving public transport and fixing roads to get you home sooner", and campaigns on the new Croydon Station and added train services on the Belgrave and Lilydale lines.

sources.title: Richard Farr — Our Team
Campaign post: Richard Farr at Croydon station on new Belgrave/Lilydale line services

victoria-2026-rachel-busbridge-eltham.summary: Advocates for "proper investment in public transport ... and active transport".

sources.title: Rachel Busbridge

victoria-2026-james-williams-essendon.summary: Supports improved cycling and public transport infrastructure.

sources.title: James Williams

victoria-2026-alysia-regan-euroa.summary: Identifies public transport gaps as a key issue affecting the regional electorate that her campaign addresses.

sources.title: Alysia Regan

victoria-2026-andrew-henley-evelyn.summary: Names access to "reliable transport" among the key services families in Evelyn are struggling with.

sources.title: Andrew Henley, Candidate for Evelyn

victoria-2026-jasmine-duff-footscray.summary: Advocates fare-free public transport and calls for government investment in public transport to address shortages in Melbourne's rapidly growing western suburbs.

sources.title: Jasmine Duff – Victorian Socialists

victoria-2026-isaac-willoughby-hastings.summary: Names neglect of public transport infrastructure in the Hastings area as a key issue that those in power have done little to address.

sources.title: Isaac Willoughby — Greens candidate for Hastings

victoria-2026-clive-crosby-hawthorn.summary: A regular public transport user, he backs half-price public transport and more train services on the Belgrave and Lilydale lines.

sources.title: Clive Crosby — Labor candidate for Hawthorn

victoria-2026-nina-crawley-ivanhoe.summary: Lists better transport among her campaign priorities for Ivanhoe.

sources.title: Nina Crawley — Greens candidate for Ivanhoe
